connected_candidates: Rollback Dry-Run replays production traffic through a candidate multi-agent workflow in a sandbox and automatically triggers rollback to the baseline upon any SLO breach, giving reliability engineers pre-deployment proof that rollback paths activate correctly and prevent incidents without manual intervention.

Affordance Gap Audit replays multi-agent conversations with synthetic ambiguous user inputs and flags every turn where the agent omits clear next-step cues, giving product designers a pre‑deployment trust map that pinpoints where missing affordances cause user friction and erode confidence.

Permission Fuzzing Replay systematically substitutes agent credentials with lower-privilege tokens during replayed multi-agent handoffs and diff‑flags any tool call that succeeds beyond the substituted scope, giving security reviewers auditable, tamper‑evident proof that every agent’s access boundary is correctly enforced before deployment.

Retention Stress Test replays multi-agent learner conversations with injected forgetting delays and misconception probes, measuring whether subsequent agents reconstruct coherent explanations; this gives learning scientists auditable proof that the workflow’s feedback loops build durable understanding before it reaches learners.

Token Budget Simulator replays captured multi-agent workflows through a pricing model that forecasts per-request token cost, latency, and throughput at scale, giving cost analysts a pre-deployment map of budget overruns and high-waste handoffs to trim before going live.
isolated_candidates: Recovery Drill: Injects simulated tool-call failures such as timeouts or permission denials into a sandboxed multi-agent run, then live-traces fallback, retry, and escalation paths. This gives reliability engineers pre-deployment proof that the workflow gracefully contains failures and prevents cascading outages.

Friction Trace: records every agent-to-agent handoff, flags repeated clarification loops, retries, and stalled threads, and overlays them as a timeline heatmap so product designers can quickly spot and remove conversational friction patterns that erode user trust before deployment.

Privilege Drift Snapshot  
Mechanism: Records every agent’s permission request and the associated rationale in a sandboxed run, then diffracts those against a declared least-privilege policy to flag deviations.  
User benefit: Gives security reviewers a single view of where agents seek or exercise excess access, enabling least-privilege enforcement and a clear audit trail before production.

Feedback Loop Sanity Check: It replays agent conversations while randomly masking an agent’s corrective explanations to test whether the collective can still converge on an accurate, durable understanding; this reveals fragility in the workflow’s feedback loops where a single missing correction could cause lasting misconceptions.

Cost-Throttle Replay: by replaying multi-agent traces under scaled-up request loads and configurable rate limits, it reveals the token cost of latency-induced backpressure and agent retries, enabling cost analysts to right-size provisioned throughput and set per-run token ceilings that meet deployment SLAs.
